rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. TRINITY PROPERTIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Trinity Management Services And Trinity Properties'S Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 15, 2012, Line 2, Defendants Trinity Management Services And Trinity Properties'S Motion For Protective Order Re Discovery, Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. It would be helpful to the court if the parties were prepared to discuss the following issues: (1) Does plaintiff have enough evidence to move for class certification? (2) How many move-out checklists are responsive to plaintiff's request for all of Trinity Properties move-out checklists for properties managed by Trinity Management? (2) if plaintiff does not have enough evidence to move for class certification and the request for all move-out checklists is unduly burdensome, how could the requests for production be narrowed? =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: JAYESH PATIL VS. DIRECTOR OF THE DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Notice Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 15, 2012, Line 3, PETITIONER JAYESH PATIL's Hearing on petition for Writ Of Mandate. Denied. This court does not have jurisdiction to review Hearing Officer Robles' denial of Petitioner's request for a continuance because his application for review was not timely per Government Code section 11524. Officer Sysum's unsigned reports are admissible. (MacDonald v. Gutierrez (2004) 32 Cal.4th 150, 159.) Respondent established that Petitioner was lawfully arrested and properly admonished. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MCLENNON LAW CORPORATION VS. ESTHER HWANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 15, 2012, Line 6, DEFENDANT ESTHER HWANG's Petition To Compel Arbitration. Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff must arbitrate the breach of contract claims against Defendant Hwang. The rest of the case is stayed pending arbitration. Plaintiff is not compelled to arbitrate its claims against Defendant De La Pena & Holiday, LLP. There is no arbitration agreement between these parties and the doctrine of equitable estoppel does not apply because Plaintiff's claims against Defendant De La Pena do not arise from the contract and can be determined independent of the issues raised by the contractual claims. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M SAGONOWSKY VS. CHRISTINE TOUR-SARKISSIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Anti-Strike Motion To Strike X-Complainants Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 15, 2012, Line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT BRADLEY WHITE's Special Anti-Strike Motion To Strike cross-Complainant's Cross Complaint. Denied, in part. Cross-Defendant has not met his threshold showing to satisfy the first prong of Code of Civil Procedure section § 425.16 as to the 12th - 15th causes of action of the Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's statements made in order to retain Cross-Complainants as counsel did not "occur in connection with 'an issue under consideration or review' in the [underlying actions]." (Blackburn v. Brady (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 670, 677; see White Decl., 7, 8) Cross-Defendant's representation of Plaintiff in the instant action and other matters do not pertain to the allegations in the Cross-Complaint. Further, the statements are not protected by the litigation privilege. Cross-Defendant did not make the statements in contemplation of litigation against Cross-Complainants. Rather, Cross-Defendant made the statements in negotiations and to persuade Cross-Complainants to represent Plaintiff in the underlying actions. (Haneline Pacific Properties, LLC v. May (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 311, 319.) Cross-Defendant is entitled to attorney fees as to the 16th cause of action for indemnity only as Cross-Complainants dismissed this cause of action in filing their First Amended Cross-Complaint. (Sylmar Air Conditioning v. Pueblo Contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049, 1056.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EXYGY, LLC VS. ANDREW MCMANUS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 15, 2012, Line 14, DEFENDANT ANDREW MCMANUS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first cause of action, and sustained, with 15 days leave to amend, as to the second cause of action. It is not clear from the pleadings that Defendant was acting as the agent for Great Nonprofits. (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal.3d 1, 24.) For the second cause of action, Plaintiff concedes that it is attempting to plead a cause of action for intentional interference with prospective economic relations, and not negligent interference with prospective business relations. (Opposition, p. 8:4-6; Complaint, 25 - 33.) Therefore, Plaintiff is given leave to amend, if it can do so in good faith, to allege each element of this cause of action, including wrongful conduct. (CACI no. 2202; San Jose Const., Inc. v. S.B.C.C., Inc. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1528, 1544-1545.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
